title: Pricing policy
content: Public pricing is not committed by the agent. Exact pricing depends on scope, team size, and integration requirements, and must be confirmed by a sales representative. Do not quote any figures.

title: ROI and performance guarantee policy
content: ROI estimates and performance guarantees must be validated by a sales engineer with access to the customer's actual data. The agent cannot commit to specific metrics, timelines, or outcomes.

title: Customer reference policy
content: Named customer references require approval from the reference team. The agent may describe general use cases and product capabilities, but may not confirm or deny any named customer relationship.

title: Manufacturing sales AI use case
content: Common pain points in manufacturing B2B sales: slow lead response time (>24h), missed follow-up windows, inconsistent CRM hygiene, and uneven prioritization across sales reps. Sales AI automates lead scoring, response reminders, CRM note generation, and next-step recommendations.

title: Sales AI product overview
content: The AI sales assistant qualifies inbound leads, asks discovery questions, books demo appointments, and writes structured CRM notes. It does not make pricing commitments, legal promises, or named customer claims.

title: SaaS company sales AI use case
content: SaaS companies use sales AI to handle high inbound volume with small SDR teams: auto-qualify website leads, route high-intent prospects to AEs, and reduce time from first touch to demo by 40-60% on average (varies by pipeline).
